items.title: UniDrive: A Unified Vision-Language and Grounding Framework for Interpretable Risk Understanding in Autonomous Driving
items.topic: UniDrive: A Unified Vision-Language and Grounding Framework for Interpretable Risk Understanding in Autonomous Driving
items.summary: The UniDrive framework, proposed by arXiv researchers, combines a temporal reasoning branch with a high-resolution perception branch to provide interpretable risk understanding in autonomous driving, addressing the trade-off between temporal reasoning and spatial precision. This approach preserves fine-grained spatial details and models scene dynamics from multi-frame visual input. UniDrive's unified visual-language and grounding framework enables more accurate and reliable autonomous driving scene understanding. The framework is particularly critical in safety-sensitive domains such as autonomous driving.
items.abstract: The UniDrive framework, proposed by arXiv researchers, combines a temporal reasoning branch with a high-resolution perception branch to provide interpretable risk understanding in autonomous driving, addressing the trade-off between temporal reasoning and spatial precision. This approach preserves fine-grained spatial details and models scene dynamics from multi-frame visual input. UniDrive's unified visual-language and grounding framework enables more accurate and reliable autonomous driving scene understanding. The framework is particularly critical in safety-sensitive domains such as autonomous driving.

items.title: RT-VLA: Real-Time Vision-Language-Action Models via Knowledge Distillation
items.topic: RT-VLA: Real-Time Vision-Language-Action Models via Knowledge Distillation
items.summary: Researchers from arXiv have proposed RT-VLA, a lightweight, distilled Vision-Language-Action model that transfers the driving and reasoning capabilities of the SimLingo model into a compact student through multi-level supervised distillation. RT-VLA preserves language-based reasoning and supports post-hoc explanation through offline language analysis of safety-critical driving moments without adding latency to real-time control. This approach enables the deployment of VLA models in real-time autonomous driving applications. RT-VLA achieves substantial inference latency reduction while maintaining the driving and reasoning capabilities of the original model.
items.abstract: Researchers from arXiv have proposed RT-VLA, a lightweight, distilled Vision-Language-Action model that transfers the driving and reasoning capabilities of the SimLingo model into a compact student through multi-level supervised distillation. RT-VLA preserves language-based reasoning and supports post-hoc explanation through offline language analysis of safety-critical driving moments without adding latency to real-time control. This approach enables the deployment of VLA models in real-time autonomous driving applications. RT-VLA achieves substantial inference latency reduction while maintaining the driving and reasoning capabilities of the original model.

items.title: Intend, Reflect, Refine: An Adaptive Multimodal Reflection Framework for Autonomous Driving
items.topic: Intend, Reflect, Refine: An Adaptive Multimodal Reflection Framework for Autonomous Driving
items.summary: The Intend, Reflect, Refine (IRR-Drive) framework, proposed by arXiv researchers, is an adaptive multimodal reflection framework for autonomous driving that generates a preliminary textual intention and anticipates potential interactions by predicting future semantic bird's-eye view representations. IRR-Drive tightly couples high-level reasoning with physical constraints, enabling more reliable and interpretable autonomous driving. This framework addresses the limitation of existing approaches that directly generate the final trajectory without explicitly examining its future consequences. IRR-Drive's dual-modality reflection space allows for more effective and safe autonomous driving decision-making.
items.abstract: The Intend, Reflect, Refine (IRR-Drive) framework, proposed by arXiv researchers, is an adaptive multimodal reflection framework for autonomous driving that generates a preliminary textual intention and anticipates potential interactions by predicting future semantic bird's-eye view representations. IRR-Drive tightly couples high-level reasoning with physical constraints, enabling more reliable and interpretable autonomous driving. This framework addresses the limitation of existing approaches that directly generate the final trajectory without explicitly examining its future consequences. IRR-Drive's dual-modality reflection space allows for more effective and safe autonomous driving decision-making.
